a: Create 3D content and incorporate it into your app’s interface.
Find out how to bring the new material to your app.
Add motion to your app’s interface to entertain or provide feedback.
Extend the reach of your app to other parts of the system.
Choose a programming approach to build your app, create your app’s interface, and implement the fundamental behaviors that your app requires.
Adopt intelligent features, like Writing Tools and Genmoji, and help people search with Visual Intelligence.
Add intelligent features with Apple Intelligence, machine learning, and related technologies.
Play, record, and process audio, and find and play content from Apple Music and someone’s music library.
Support audio and video content in your app.

t: Building your macOS game remotely from your PC
a: Configure a Mac for remote builds, and use it to build your game from your PC and catch mistakes when porting your game to macOS.
Analyze photos, videos, speech, sound, and text using the models built in to the system frameworks.
Provide timely information and keep people connected to their friends using technologies like push notifications, Siri, and SharePlay.
Make it easier for people to access their content and adopt your app as part of their daily workflow.
Embrace features that improve people’s experience with your app on Apple devices.
Build your app’s data model, persist that data to disk or iCloud, and access people’s personal data.
Discover the tools, programming languages, and resources you use to develop apps on Apple platforms.
Adjust your app’s behavior using contextual data you receive from a device’s built-in sensors.
Distribute your apps on the App Store, or make them available to people directly from your website.
Draw custom content in your app’s views, and print content to a file or available printer.
Navigate the file system on Apple devices, find important directories, and read and write your app’s documents and files.
Plan the creation of your game and incorporate the gameplay features people expect.
Deliver fantastic game experiences to millions of players worldwide using Apple hardware, graphics, audio, social gaming, and distribution tools.
Enhance features in your app by using the models at the core of Apple Intelligence.
Create 2D and 3D content for your app, and learn how to animate and print that content.
Access device-related sensors, connect to a network, and build low-level drivers and software to support custom hardware.
Communicate with connected hardware and write code that runs well on Apple silicon.
Display existing images and photos, create or capture new images, and read and write image data.
Include helpful information in your interface and make it easy for people to find your app’s content.
Explore the components that go into building your app’s interface, and discover platform-specific features that improve the experience you offer to people.
Learn how to design and develop beautiful interfaces that leverage Liquid Glass.
Stream audio and video content to a device, or to AirPlay-enabled hardware.
Communicate with other devices over a network, extend the system’s core networking capabilities, and incorporate telephony into your apps.
Access the personal data that people keep on their devices.
Share data with your apps running on different devices using iCloud, or share data between your app and app extensions.
Create immersive video experiences in visionOS.
Store fundamental types of data, and discover the key behaviors that make using those types easier.
Build a structured data model for your app, and persist that data model to disk or iCloud.
Build your app for all Apple platforms using the Swift programming language and a modern approach.
Learn about the wide range of technologies you use to develop software for Apple platforms.
Discover and fix potential issues in your code by testing and regularly collecting performance metrics.
Display localized text from your app’s interface, and discover how to lay out and render text yourself.
Create software on Apple platforms using Xcode and the Swift programming language, and distribute your apps on the App Store.
Build your app using a traditional design approach and the Swift or Objective-C programming language.
Build video playback, capture, and editing apps that support advanced system features.
